working_definition: A rare early dermal fibroblast in prenatal skin and hair-bearing skin organoid (SkO) that expresses the Wnt antagonist FRZB. Primarily observed at 7 PCW; excluded from fibroblast trajectory analysis due to its rarity in prenatal skin, though FRZB-expressing fibroblasts are present in several other developing organs.

best_match.cl_definition: Any skin fibroblast that is part of some dermis.

justification: CL:0002551 (fibroblast of dermis) is the closest available term for this rare early dermal fibroblast. No CL term exists for an early, FRZB-expressing, or progenitor-state dermal fibroblast. The population resides in the dermis of prenatal skin and SkO but is defined by a specific marker (FRZB) and developmental stage not captured by any existing CL term, making this a broad match.

other_candidates.cl_definition: A connective tissue cell which secretes an extracellular matrix rich in collagen and other macromolecules.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: Too general; no skin or dermis specificity.
Less specific than fibroblast of dermis; CL:0002551 is a subclass of CL:0002620 and better captures the dermal location.

other_candidates.cl_definition: A specialized dermal fibroblast within the hair follicle mesenchymal compartment that participates in epithelial-mesenchymal signaling to regulate hair morphogenesis, cycling, and homeostasis through production of inductive signals and extracellular matrix maintenance.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: FRZB+ cells are not assigned to a hair follicle lineage; they were excluded from fibroblast trajectory analysis entirely.

recommendation: Use CL:0002551 (fibroblast of dermis) as a broad match. A new term for FRZB+ early dermal fibroblast is not warranted at this stage given the extreme rarity of this population in prenatal skin and its exclusion from trajectory analyses.
